title: A Scary Hotel Mix-Up: Stranger Enters Couple's Room
summary: A couple named Chris and Paul were staying at a Travelodge hotel in Lincoln for a fun mini-break. They had just checked in and fixed their TV when something scary happened. A stranger walked into their room with a key card given by hotel staff. The man was surprised and left quickly, but Chris felt very shaken. She went to the reception to complain, but the staff just said sorry and were not very helpful. This happened after Travelodge promised to make hotels safer because a similar incident led to an assault. Travelodge apologized and said they will retrain their staff to prevent such mistakes. It shows how important it is for hotels to keep guests safe by being careful with room keys.

background_read: Hotels like Travelodge are places where people stay for vacations or trips. They give guests key cards to enter their rooms, and it's very important that staff keep these keys safe to protect guests' privacy and safety. Sometimes, mistakes can happen, like giving a key to the wrong person, which can be scary for guests. This story shows why hotels need good rules and training to prevent such errors and keep everyone secure.
Article_Structure: WHO: Chris Adamson and her husband Paul, guests at Travelodge, and a stranger who entered their room.
WHAT: A stranger was given a key card by hotel staff and entered the couple's room, causing fear and a complaint.
WHERE: At a Travelodge hotel in Lincoln, England.
WHY: To highlight a security mistake by the hotel and the importance of safety measures after similar past incidents.

perspectives.description: The couple's experience shows how hotel errors can make guests feel unsafe and need better protection.
Travelodge apologized and promised retraining, showing they are trying to fix security issues.
